text: You can see we've got a liver retractor just sort of gently elevated in the anterior aspect of the left lobe of the liver.
The fundus and the wrap are completely stuck to the liver more superiorly.
And that's where we will begin our dissection.

This is the left cruse being retracted to the right and the esophagus, and we're moving up into the mediastinum, hoping to find some untrampled tissue that is gonna be easier to work with than what we have been dealing with.
This is just a picture demonstrating how we perform a wedge gastroplasty.
I put the bougie within the esophagus, rotate the fundus over to the left upper quadrant.
We come down with a stapler to the bougie, then retract the fundus over with the wedge up in the air, and then come along the bougie to create that neoesophagus.
Then after that's complete, this is just us performing in the wrap.
I've placed one stitch inferiorly, I'm about to place the second stitch above that, and then we'll plan to close the hiatus again.
